person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
geni.com
Constance d'Arles, Queen consort of the Franks
French: Constance d'Arles, reine consort de France
Also Known As:	"Constance de Provence", "Constance de Toulouse", "Constance de Taillefer"
Birthdate:	circa March 27, 974
Birthplace:	Arles, France
Death:	July 22, 1032 (54-62)
Château de Melun, Melun, Île-de-France, France 
Place of Burial:	Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, France
Immediate Family:	
Daughter of Guillaume II "le Liberateur" comte de Provence and Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou, Reine consort d'Aquitaine
Wife of Robert II Capet, "the Pious" king of the Franks

Mother of 
Emergarde de Auvergne; 
Hedwige de France, comtesse d'Auxerre; 
Hugues, roi associé de France; 
Henry I, king of France; 


Adela of France, countess of Flanders; Constance de France, heritiere de Dammartin; Robert I le Vieux, duc de Bourgogne and Eudes de France 

Sister of Lucia de Provence, comtesse consort de Razès; Guillaume III le Pieux, comte de Provence and Ermengarde of Arles

Half sister of Alajarde de Provence, souveraine d'Antibes; Ermengarde de Toulouse; Pons, comte de Gévaudan; Ermengarde / Humberge de Limoges de Gévaudan, [daughter of Etienne II de Gévaudan and ADelais d'Anjou]; Almodis de Limoges de Gévaudan; Eimilde de Gévaudan; Guillaume III Taillefer, comte de Toulouse and Tota de Toulouse « less
Occupation:	Grevinne, Drottning av Frankrike, Drottning av Frankrike, Queen Consort of France (1001-1031), Queen consort of the Franks, Queen of France, Queen Consort of France, Queen of the Franks, QUEEN OF FRANCE, b. abt 0986, Princess, reine de France
Constance of Arles (c. 986 - 28 July 1032), also known as Constance of Provence, was a queen consort of France as the third spouse of King Robert II of France.
Life
Born c.?986 Constance was the daughter of William I, count of Provence and Adelaide-Blanche of Anjou, daughter of Fulk II of Anjou. She was the half-sister of Count William II of Provence. Constance was married to King Robert, after his divorce from his second wife, Bertha of Burgundy. The marriage was stormy; Bertha's family opposed her, and Constance was despised for importing her Provençal kinfolk and customs. Robert's friend, Hugh of Beauvais, tried to convince the king to repudiate her in 1007. Possibly at her request twelve knights of her kinsman, Fulk Nerra, then murdered Beauvais.

In 1010 Robert went to Rome, followed by his former wife Bertha, to seek permission to divorce Constance and remarry Bertha. Pope Sergius IV was not about to allow a consanguineous marriage which had been formally condemned by Pope Gregory V and Robert had already repudiated two wives. So the request was denied. After his return according to one source Robert "loved his wife more."

In the famous trial in 1022 of members of the clergy, including Constance's previous confessor Stephen, on charges of heresy Robert had his wife Queen Constance stand at the door to prevent any mob violence. However, as the condemned clerics left the trial the queen "struck out the eye of Stephen... with the staff which she carried". This was seen as Constance venting her frustration at anyone subverting the prestige of the crown.

At Constance's urging, her eldest son Hugh Magnus was crowned co-king alongside his father in 1017. But later Hugh demanded his parents share power with him, and rebelled against his father in 1025. Constance, however, on learning of her son's rebellion was furious with him, rebuking him at every turn. At some point Hugh was reconciled with his parents but shortly thereafter died, probably about age eighteen.

Robert and Constance quarrelled over which of their surviving sons should inherit the throne; Robert favored their second son Henry, while Constance favored their third son, Robert. Despite his mother's protests and her support by several bishops, Henry was crowned in 1027. Constance, however, was not graceful when she didn't get her way. The ailing Fulbert, bishop of Chartres told a colleague that he could attend the ceremony "if he traveled slowly to Reims but he was too frightened of the queen to go at all".

Constance encouraged her sons to rebel, and they began attacking and pillaging the towns and castles belonging to their father. Son Robert attacked Burgundy, the duchy he had been promised but had never received, and Henry seized Dreux. At last King Robert agreed to their demands and peace was made which lasted until the king's death.

King Robert died on 20 July 1031. Soon afterwards Constance was at odds with both her surviving sons. Constance seized her dower lands and refused to surrender them. Henry fled to Normandy, where he received aid, weapons and soldiers from his brother Robert. He returned to besiege his mother at Poissy but Constance escaped to Pontoise. She only surrendered when Henry began the siege of Le Puiset and swore to slaughter all the inhabitants.

Constance died 28 July 1032. and was buried beside her husband Robert at Saint-Denis Basilica.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Constance d'Arles於0972年3月27日出生在法國普洛福-阿爾培-o特 達阿爾布-度-o阿爾斯時，她的父親Guillaume de Provence Comte de Provence22歲，母親Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou24歲。她於0998年9月18日與Robert II le Pieux roi de France在法國瑟-吉馬內默倫結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1032年7月25日在法國瑟-吉馬內默倫享年60歲，埋葬在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲巴西利卡 本 聖 丹尼斯。

nameMeanings.name: d'Arles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: In some cases possibly also southern French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Sarilo (see Sarlo ).

variants.name: Searles
meanings: English: variant of Searle , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Constance
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval form of the Late Latin name Constantia, which is either a feminine derivative of Constans ( see Constant ) or an abstract noun meaning ‘constancy’. This was a popular name among the Normans, and was borne by, amongst others, the formidable Constance of Sicily ( 1158–98 ), wife of the Emperor Henry VI .

en.lifeSummary: When Constance d'Arles was born on 27 March 0972, in Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France, her father, Guillaume de Provence Comte de Provence, was 22 and her mother, Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou, was 24. She married Robert II le Pieux roi de France on 18 September 0998, in Melun, Seine-et-Marne, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 25 July 1032, in Melun, Seine-et-Marne, France, at the age of 60, and was buried in Basilica of Saint Denis, Paris, Île-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Constance d'Arles est née le 27 mars 0972, à Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, France, son père, Guillaume de Provence Comte de Provence, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Robert II le Pieux roi de France le 18 septembre 0998, à Melun, Seine-et-Marne, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 25 juillet 1032, à Melun, Seine-et-Marne, France, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterrée à Basilica of Saint Denis, Paris, Île-de-France, France.

de.Basilica of Saint-Denis, Paris, Île-de-F: Basilica of Saint Denis, Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich
de.lifeSummary: Als Constance d'Arles am 27. März 0972 in Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guillaume de Provence Comte de Provence 22 und ihre Mutter Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou 24. Sie hat am 18. September 0998 in Melun, Seine-et-Marne, Frankreich Robert II le Pieux roi de France geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 25. Juli 1032 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Melun, Seine-et-Marne, Frankreich und wurde in Basilica of Saint Denis, Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich begraben.

Lei sposato Robert II le Pieux roi de France il 18 settembre 0998, nel Melun, Seine-et-Marne, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 25 luglio 1032, nel Melun, Seine-et-Marne, Francia, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolta a Basilica of Saint Denis, Paris, Île-de-France, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Constance d'Arlesが0972年3月27日にフランスプローベンセ-アルペス-クoト ド'アズルボウチェス-ドウ-ルhoンアルレスで生まれた時，父Guillaume de Provence Comte de Provenceは22歳，母Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjouは24歳であった。0998年9月18日にフランスセアイネ-エト-マルンメルンでRobert II le Pieux roi de Franceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1032年7月25日にフランスセアイネ-エト-マルンメルンで60歳で死去し，フランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスバシリカ オフ サイント デニスにて埋葬された。

ko.Basilica of Saint-Denis, Paris, Île-de-F: 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스 바실리카 프 세인트 데니스
ko.lifeSummary: Constance d'Arles(이)가 0972년 3월 27일 프랑스 플로븐스-알페스-co트 드'아저 부체스-드-ho니 알레스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guillaume de Provence Comte de Provence(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 0998년 9월 18일 프랑스 세이인-트-마니 멜루에서 Robert II le Pieux roi de France(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1032년 7월 25일 프랑스 세이인-트-마니 멜루에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스 바실리카 프 세인트 데니스에 묻혔다.

pt.27 Mar 972–25 JUL 1032: 27 de março de 0972–25 de julho de 1032
pt.Basilica of Saint-Denis, Paris, Île-de-F: Basilica of Saint Denis, Paris, Île-de-France, França
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Constance d'Arles nasceu em 27 de março de 0972, em Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, França, seu pai, Guillaume de Provence Comte de Provence, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert II le Pieux roi de France em 18 de setembro de 0998, em Melun, Seine-et-Marne, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 25 de julho de 1032, em Melun, Seine-et-Marne, França, com 60 anos, e foi sepultada em Basilica of Saint Denis, Paris, Île-de-France, França.

ro.Basilica of Saint-Denis, Paris, Île-de-F: Basilica of Saint Denis, Paris, Île-de-France, Franţa

ru.Basilica of Saint-Denis, Paris, Île-de-F: басилика оф сеинт денис, пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Constance d'Arles родилась 27 марта 0972 г. в местности арльс, боучес-ду-рoн, провенсе-алпес-кoт д'азур, Франция, её отцу по имени Guillaume de Provence Comte de Provence было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert II le Pieux roi de France 18 сентября 0998 г. в местности мелун, сине-ет-марн, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 25 июля 1032 г. в местности мелун, сине-ет-марн, Франция в возрасте 60 лет и была похоронена в местности басилика оф сеинт денис, пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция.

es.27 Mar 972–25 JUL 1032: 27 de marzo de 0972–25 de julio de 1032
es.Basilica of Saint-Denis, Paris, Île-de-F: Basilica of Saint Denis, Paris, Île-de-France, Francia
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Constance d'Arles nació el 27 de marzo de 0972, en Arles, Bouches-du-Rhône, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur, Francia, su papá, Guillaume de Provence Comte de Provence, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Adélaïde la Blanche d'Anjou, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Robert II le Pieux roi de France el 18 de septiembre de 0998, en Melun, Seine-et-Marne, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 25 de julio de 1032, en Melun, Seine-et-Marne, Francia, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultada en Basilica of Saint Denis, Paris, Île-de-France, Francia.
